tasks.spec: Create the KV namespace binding and wrangler.jsonc config for the URL shortener Worker. The Worker needs a KV namespace called LINKS to store slug→URL mappings.

locked_tests.assertion: Given wrangler.jsonc, When parsed, Then it parses without error and the LINKS binding is present in kv_namespaces
Given the SlugEntry type, When a value is constructed, Then it requires slug, url, and created_at fields

tasks.spec: Implement the core Worker handler: POST /shorten to create a slug→URL mapping in KV; GET /:slug to redirect; 404 for unknown slugs. Validate URL format before storing.

resolved_judgments.url_validation: URL constructor must not throw; http and https only

locked_tests.assertion: Given a valid URL, When POST /shorten, Then status 201 and body {slug, short_url} where short_url ends with slug
Given a stored slug, When GET /:slug, Then status 301 with Location header equal to the original URL
Given an unknown slug, When GET /:slug, Then status 404 with body {error} (not 500, not empty 200)
Given an invalid URL, When POST /shorten, Then status 400 with body {error}
Given a non-http/https URL, When POST /shorten, Then status 400
Given a URL longer than 2048 chars, When POST /shorten, Then status 400

tasks.spec: Add rate limiting on POST /shorten: max 10 requests per IP per minute using Cloudflare Rate Limiting binding. Block with 429 and Retry-After header. Also add a DELETE /:slug admin endpoint protected by a Bearer token from env.

resolved_judgments.rate_limit_key: IP from cf.connectingIp or x-forwarded-for first segment

locked_tests.assertion: Given 10 prior POST /shorten from one IP within 60s, When an 11th arrives, Then status 429 with header Retry-After: 60
Given a valid Bearer token, When DELETE /:slug, Then status 204 and the slug is no longer readable via GET /:slug
Given no Authorization header, When DELETE /:slug, Then status 401
Given a wrong token, When DELETE /:slug, Then status 401
Given an unknown slug with a valid token, When DELETE /:slug, Then status 404
Given the admin token check, When compared, Then a timing-safe comparison is used (crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual)
